trade.summary: Modest bilateral trade. Canada exports wheat, canola, coal, and mining equipment to Indonesia. Indonesia exports textiles, electronics, palm oil, rubber, and seafood to Canada.

diplomatic.summary: Constructive but not deeply engaged bilateral relations. Canada and Indonesia cooperate in APEC and G20. Canada has development assistance programs in Indonesia focusing on governance and gender equality. Both share interest in rule-based international order.

military.summary: Limited but growing defense cooperation. Canada has provided some maritime security training to Indonesia. No arms transfers or joint exercises of significance.

financial.summary: Canadian mining companies (Barrick, Agnico-Eagle adjacent firms) have interests in Indonesian mining. Indonesian investment in Canada is very limited.

energy.summary: Limited energy trade. Indonesia exports coal to Canada. Canada has exported some clean energy technology. Growing interest in critical minerals cooperation.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Canada funds some research programs in Indonesia through IDRC. Academic exchanges exist but are not extensive.
